rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place component type annexes in the component type scope and implementation annexes in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current target component or its type for AGREE expressions
Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the most specific valid scope for the contract
Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting annex blocks
Use only visible identifiers from the current target component or its type for AGREE expressions
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not place AGREE annex blocks in invalid scopes or before required subcomponents and connections
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
